description: Length-matched elliptic curve facts for oracle verification.

facts.context: Hasse's theorem bounds the number of points on E(F_p). For p=100, the bound is approximately:
distractors: p ± √p, so roughly 90 to 110 points
p+1 ± p/2, so roughly 51 to 151 points
2p ± √p, so roughly 190 to 210 points

facts.context: The discriminant of an elliptic curve y² = x³ + ax + b equals:
distractors: Δ = -(4a³ + 27b²) only
Δ = -4a³ - 27b² exactly
Δ = a² - 4b (quadratic form)

facts.context: An elliptic curve is singular (not actually an elliptic curve) when:
distractors: either a = 0 or b = 0 in Weierstrass form
the j-invariant takes an undefined value here
the curve possesses no rational points at all

facts.context: The j-invariant of y² = x³ + ax + b equals 1728 when:
facts.truth: b = 0, giving curves of the form y² = x³ + ax
distractors: a = 0, giving curves of the form y² = x³ + b
a = b holds, making the curve self-dual here
4a³ = 27b² holds at the cuspidal boundary

facts.context: By Mazur's theorem, the torsion subgroup of E(Q) can have order:
distractors: any integer from 1 to 12 without restrictions
only prime orders up to 11 and their products
any order that divides 12 by Lagrange's theorem

facts.context: The BSD conjecture has been proven for elliptic curves:
distractors: of any rank over the rational number field Q
only for curves with complex multiplication (CM)
with good reduction at all prime numbers p > 2

facts.context: In point doubling on y² = x³ + ax + b, the slope m equals:
distractors: (y₂ - y₁) / (x₂ - x₁)
2y / (3x² + a) inverted
(3x² + b) / (2y) using b

facts.context: The group E(F_p) for an elliptic curve over a prime field is always:
facts.truth: Z/nZ or Z/n₁Z × Z/n₂Z where n₂ | n₁ and n₂ | p-1
distractors: cyclic of order dividing p-1 by Fermat's theorem
isomorphic to a multiplicative subgroup of (Z/pZ)*
of order exactly p+1 by the Hasse bound equality

facts.context: A point P on an elliptic curve has order 2 (2-torsion) if and only if:
facts.truth: y = 0, meaning the point lies on the x-axis
distractors: x = 0, meaning the point lies on the y-axis
it equals the identity element O at infinity
P = -P holds with P not equal to the origin

facts.context: The Mordell-Weil theorem states that for E/Q:
facts.truth: E(Q) is a finitely generated abelian group
distractors: E(Q) is finite with bounded cardinality
E(Q) has rank bounded above by 28
E(Q) is always a cyclic group structure

facts.context: The curve y² = x³ + x over F_p is supersingular when:
facts.truth: p ≡ 3 (mod 4), so -1 is not a QR mod p
distractors: p ≡ 1 (mod 4), so -1 is a quadratic residue
p = 2 only, the unique even prime characteristic
for all odd primes p regardless of congruence

facts.context: Two elliptic curves over the same field are isomorphic if and only if:
distractors: they have the same discriminant Δ
they have the same number of points
one transforms to the other by linear change
